subtitle.rechallenge: Rematch setup is prefilled from your finished debate.
subtitle.challenge: This debate is invite-only until they accept.

banners.challenge: You are creating an invite-only challenge for @{username}. While waiting, this debate stays private to both of you.
banners.prefill: Rechallenge mode is on. Setup fields are prefilled from the finished debate.
banners.newTopic: New-topic challenge mode is on. Your opponent is fixed from the finished debate.
banners.limit: You’ve reached the 5-active-debate limit. Finish or forfeit one to create another.
banners.verify: Email verification required for creating debates. Go to Settings to verify your email.

setup.title: Debate setup
setup.topicPlaceholder: e.g. Is X always preferable to Y, or does context determine the answer?
setup.contextPlaceholder: Set the terms for your challenger -- your preferred pace, how you're defining key terms, and any background context they should know before diving in.
setup.contextHelp: Visible in the header as context for both debaters.

opening.title: Opening argument
opening.placeholder:
The common view is that X is straightforwardly good, but this misses something important. The *actual* evidence points the other way -- when you look at the data, the effect is either negligible or reversed in most contexts.

Critics will say Y, but **that argument assumes Z**, which hasn’t held up. As one study put it:

> "The correlation disappears entirely once you control for confounding variable W." [1]

The stronger case is that...

[1] https://example.com/source
opening.help: {min}–{max} characters. Lead with a claim, then the reasoning behind it. Sources are welcome — plain text is fine.
opening.aiAssisted: I used AI assistance for this turn
opening.aiNotePlaceholder: Briefly describe how AI contributed — e.g. suggested sources, restructured a paragraph.
